ict-123/equations-and-math/laws/kirchhof.title: Kirchhoff's Law
ict-123/equations-and-math/laws/kirchhof.description:
Kirchhoff's current law
Total current going into a junction = the total amount going out
I = I1 + I2 +…
Kirchhoff's voltage law
Kirchhoff's voltage law states that, given any two specific points on a circuit at a particular time, the difference in voltage between the two points (known as the voltage drop) is the same no matter what pathway you take to get there.

ict-128/scripting/example-scripts/cleanu.description:
#Cleanup-OldFiles.ps1
#Author Skulky Tim
#modifyed by Ryder Fryling
#Created February 27, 2019
#Last Modified mar 14, 2026 #Rationale and Synopsis
#The intention is to locate all the files not used within a specified date in a current directory.
#Once the files are found, user should be given the chance to delete them.
#Current status: Kind of sort of halfish done, but execution not working as expected
#It would also be nice if user could select how old the files should be (e.g. older than 30 days, 10 days, 60 days, etc.) #Variables
$days = Read-Host "enter how many days ago you would like to search" $filepath = (Get-Location)
$olderthandate = (Get-Date).AddDays(-$days) #Execution #creates the table Get-ChildItem -Path $filepath -Recurse | Where-Object {$_.LastWriteTime -le $olderthandate} |
Format-Table -Property Name,LastWriteTime #decision to delete the files if ((Read-Host "delete the Files?") -contains "yes" -or "y") { #gets the files and deletes them Get-ChildItem -Path $filepath -Recurse | Where-Object {$_.LastWriteTime -le $olderthandate} | remove-item -WhatIf }else{ #seccond option saying the files wernt deleted Write-Host "the files wre not deleted" } <# .SYNOPSIS The intention is to locate all the files not used within a specified date in a current directory. .DESCRIPTION The intention is to locate all the files not used within a specified date in a current directory. Once the files are found, user should be given the chance to delete them. Current status: Kind of sort of halfish done, but execution not working as expected It would also be nice if user could select how old the files should be (e.g. older than 30 days, 10 days, 60 days, etc.)
#>

ict-128/scripting/example-scripts/get-ci.title: Get CIM Computer Info
ict-128/scripting/example-scripts/get-ci.description:
#Get-CIMComputerInfo.ps1
#Author: Ryder Fryling #Created: 2026/03/28
#Version 1.0
#Updated: N/A
#Description: this script gatheres information from a inputted list of computers and compiles the collected information in a CSV file. <#
.SYNOPSIS this script gatheres information from a inputted list of computers and compiles the collected information in a CSV file. .DESCRIPTION
this script gatheres information from a inputted list of computers and compiles the collected information in a CSV file. it will only create the file when a location is given specifying where the file is to be located. .PARAMETER InfoClass
this is the type of information that is to be gethered. only one type can be gathered at a time .PARAMETER Device
you can input a list of devices to gether information from. this has to be a string. .PARAMETER OutFile_Path
This parameter specifyes the location of where the CSV file is to be created. it is also the switch for the creation of a CSV file. .EXAMPLE
PS C:\Users\bob\Desktop> .\Get-CIMComputerInfo.ps1 -Device DESKTOP-GQ327M5,LocalHost -InfoClass LogicalDisk -OutFile_Path .\ Win32_LogicalDisk: C: (DeviceID = "C:") Win32_LogicalDisk: D: (DeviceID = "D:") DeviceID DriveType ProviderName VolumeName Size FreeSpace PSComputerName -------- --------- ------------ ---------- ---- --------- -------------- C: 3 135406809088 109951479808 DESKTOP-GQ327M5
D: 5 CPBA_X64FRE_EN-US_DV9 6969382912 0 DESKTOP-GQ327M5
C: 3 135406809088 109951479808 LocalHost D: 5 CPBA_X64FRE_EN-US_DV9 6969382912 0 LocalHost LastWriteTime : 3/29/2026 11:51:01 PM
Length : 0
Name : WS-Info-CIM-LogicalDisk-2026-51-29.csv .EXAMPLE
PS C:\Users\bob\Desktop> .\Get-CIMComputerInfo.ps1 -Device LocalHost -InfoClass BIOS -Start_RM SMBIOSBIOSVersion Manufacturer Name SerialNumber Version PSComputerName
----------------- ------------ ---- ------------ ------- --------------
Hyper-V UEFI Release v4.1 Microsoft Corporation Hyper-V UEFI Release v4.1 8546-1777-1739-7800-3950-9116-75 VRTUAL - 1 LocalHost #>
param( [ValidateSet("BIOS", "OS", "Service", "LogicalDisk")] [string] $InfoClass = "LogicalDisk", [array] $Device = "LocalHost", [parameter(Mandatory = $false)] [string] $OutFile_Path, [parameter(Mandatory = $false)] [switch] $Start_RM ) #matches the class to the inputed parameter if ($InfoClass -eq "BIOS") { $InfoClass_var = "CIM_BIOSElement" } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "OS"){ $InfoClass_var = "CIM_OperatingSystem" } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "Service"){ $InfoClass_var = "CIM_Service" } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "LogicalDisk"){ $InfoClass_var = "CIM_LogicalDisk"} #starts WinRM on the local host if needed if ($Start_RM){ Start-Service -Name WinRM } #creates terminal output $Device | ForEach-Object { Get-CimInstance -ComputerName $_ -ClassName $InfoClass_var |Format-Table -AutoSize -Wrap
} #creates the outfile if seleced if ($OutFile_Path) { #creates the file name and full path $File_Name = "WS-Info-CIM-$($InfoClass)-$(Get-Date -Format yyyy-mm-dd).csv" $OutFile_Path_compleate = $OutFile_Path + "\" + $File_Name #crteates the file New-Item -Path $OutFile_Path -Name $File_Name -ItemType File #loops through th inputted devices and adds it to the file $Device | ForEach-Object{ Get-CimInstance -ComputerName $_ -ClassName $InfoClass_var |Export-Csv -Path $OutFile_Path_compleate -NoTypeInformation -Append } }

ict-128/scripting/example-scripts/get-di.title: Get Disk Report
ict-128/scripting/example-scripts/get-di.description:
#Get-DiskReport.ps1
#Author: Ryder Fryling #Created: 2026/03/19
#Version 1.0
#Updated: N/A
#Description: This script reports disk space for one or more drives, with output format controlled by a parameter. <#
.SYNOPSIS
This script reports disk space for one or more drives .DESCRIPTION
This script reports disk space for one or more drives, with output format controlled by a parameter to set the measurment in GB or MB. #>
param( [string[]]$driveletter, [Validateset("GB", "MB")] [string]$unit = "GB"
) #formates the user input to match powershell input $DataUnit = if ($unit -eq "GB") { 1GB } else { 1MB } #collects the data Get-Volume -DriveLetter $driveletter | ForEach-Object{ [PSCustomObject]@{ #formates the table with some math Drive = $_.DriveLetter "Total($unit)" = [math]::Round($_.Size / $DataUnit, 2) "Free($unit)" = [math]::Round($_.SizeRemaining / $DataUnit, 2 ) PercentFree = [math]::Round(($_.SizeRemaining / $_.Size) * 100, 2) } }|Format-Table -AutoSize

ict-128/scripting/example-scripts/get-se.title: Get Service Health Report
ict-128/scripting/example-scripts/get-se.description:
#Get-ServiceHealthReport.ps1
#Author: Ryder Fryling #Created: 2026/03/22
#Version 1.0
#Updated: N/A
#Description: This script Audits Windows services by status, startup type, and service account. <#
.SYNOPSIS Audits Windows services by status, startup type, and service account. .DESCRIPTION Queries Windows services using Get-Service and Get-CimInstance, then filters and reports based on the specified criteria. Useful for identifying stopped auto-start services, services running under non-standard accounts, or summarizing overall service health. .PARAMETER Status Filter services by status. Valid values: Running, Stopped, All. Defaults to All. .PARAMETER StartupType Filter by startup type. Valid values: Automatic, Manual, Disabled, All. Defaults to All. .PARAMETER AccountFilter If specified, shows only services running under this account name (partial match). For example, "LocalSystem" or "Network". .PARAMETER SortBy Property to sort results by. Valid values: Name, Status, StartType. Defaults to Name. .EXAMPLE .\Get-ServiceHealthReport.ps1 Lists all services sorted by name. .EXAMPLE .\Get-ServiceHealthReport.ps1 -Status Stopped -StartupType Automatic Shows only stopped services that are configured to start automatically (potential problems that need attention). .EXAMPLE .\Get-ServiceHealthReport.ps1 -AccountFilter "LocalSystem" -SortBy Status Shows services running as LocalSystem, sorted by their current status.
#>
param( [validateSet("Running", "Stopped", "All")] [string]$Status = "All", [validateSet("Automatic", "Manual", "Disabled", "Auto", "All")] [string]$StartupType = "All", [Parameter(Mandatory = $False)] [string]$AccountFilter, [validateSet("Name", "Status", "StartType", "StartMode", "State")] [string]$SortBy = "Name" ) #gets the services $services = Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_Service #sorts them by status
if ($Status -ne "All") { $services = $services | Where-Object { $_.State -eq $Status } } #sorts them by start type if ($StartupType -ne "All") { $services = $services | Where-Object { $_.StartMode -eq $StartupType } } #filters the accounts to best match user input if ($AccountFilter) { $services = $services | Where-Object { $_.StartName -like "*$AccountFilter*" } } #formats the $SortBy to be valid input options if ($SortBy -eq "Status") { $SortBy = "State" } elseif ($SortBy -eq "StartType") { $SortBy = "StartMode" } #sorts the data then makes the table and formats it $services | Sort-Object -Property $SortBy | ForEach-Object { [PSCustomObject]@{ Name = $_.Name DisplayName = $_.DisplayName Status = $_.State StartType = $_.StartMode Account = $_.StartName } } | Format-Table -Property Name,Status,StartType,Account,DisplayName -AutoSize -Wrap

ict-128/scripting/example-scripts/get-wm.title: Get WMI Computer Info
ict-128/scripting/example-scripts/get-wm.description:
#Get-WMIComputerInfo.ps1
#Author: Ryder Fryling #Created: 2026/03/28
#Version 1.0
#Updated: N/A
#Description: this script gathers diffrent WMI info from a computer specifyed by the user <#
.SYNOPSIS this script gathers diffrent WMI info from a computer specifyed by the user .DESCRIPTION this script gathers diffrent WMI info from a computer specifyed by the user. i can also creat a file in a specifyed location if needed. it will also format some of the data. .PARAMETERInfoClass
this is the type of WMI information being collected .PARAMETER Device the device that the information is being collected from .PARAMETER OutFile_Path
the swithc for creating a file with the information as well as the path of its location. .EXAMPLE PS C:\Users\bob\Desktop> .\Get-WMIComputerInfo.ps1 -InfoClass LogicalDisk -Device DESKTOP-GQ327M5 DeviceID DriveType ProviderName FreeSpace Size VolumeName -------- --------- ------------ --------- ---- ---------- C: 3 109947891712 135406809088 D: 5 0 6969382912 CPBA_X64FRE_EN-US_DV9 .EXAMPLE
PS C:\Users\bob\Desktop> .\Get-WMIComputerInfo.ps1 -OutFile_Path .\ SMBIOSBIOSVersion Manufacturer Name SerialNumber Version ----------------- ------------ ---- ------------ ------- Hyper-V UEFI Release v4.1 Microsoft Corporation Hyper-V UEFI Release v4.1 8546-1777-1739-7800-3950-9116-75 VRTUAL - 1 #>
param( [ValidateSet("BIOS", "OS", "Service", "LogicalDisk")] [string] $InfoClass = "BIOS", [string] $Device = "LocalHost", [parameter(Mandatory = $false)] [string] $OutFile_Path )
#sets the variable for the type of data being collected if ($InfoClass -eq "BIOS") { $InfoClass_var = "Win32_Bios" } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "OS"){ $InfoClass_var = "Win32_OperatingSystem" } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "Service"){ $InfoClass_var = "Win32_Service" } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "LogicalDisk"){ $InfoClass_var = "Win32_LogicalDisk" } #creates the formatting for the teble and data
if ($InfoClass -eq "BIOS"){ $Table_Data = Select-Object -Property SMBIOSBIOSVersion,Name,Version,SerialNumber,Manufacturer } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "OS"){ $Table_Data = Select-Object -Property SystemDirectory,Organization,BuildNumber,SerialNumber,Version } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "Service"){ $Table_Data = Select-Object -Property Name,ProccessID,StartMode,State } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "LogicalDisk"){ $Table_Data = Select-Object -Property DeviceID,@{name='FreeSpace';expression={[Math]::Round($_.FreeSpace / 1MB, 3)}}, @{name='Size';expression={[math]::Round($_.Size / 1GB, 3)}}, @{name='%Free';expression={[Math]::Round($_.FreeSpace / $_.Size * 100, 3)}} } #collects info $Device_Data = Get-WmiObject -Class $InfoClass_var -ComputerName $Device |Select-Object $Table_Data #creates file name and full path
$File_Name = "$($Device)-$($InfoClass)-$(Get-Date -Format yyyy-mm-dd).csv"
$OutFile_Path_compleate = $OutFile_Path + "\" + $File_Name #creates file if specifyed if ($OutFile_Path) { Get-WmiObject -Class $InfoClass_var -ComputerName $Device |Select-Object $Table_Data |Export-Csv -Path $OutFile_Path_compleate } #prints data to the terminal $Device_Data |Format-Table -AutoSize -Wrap

ict-128/scripting/example-scripts/import.title: Import-ADUser
ict-128/scripting/example-scripts/import.description:
# date created: 05/03/2026
# date modified: 16/03/2026
# author: Erik Bjerstedt
#
# Synopsis: automatically creates AD users on the system from a CSV file
#
# Warning: This script assumes that your OU structure and naming (including capitalization) is identical to the project's ADDS diagram.
# Before running, verify that your OUs are structured correctly.
#
# Citations:
# ICT 128 Lab 07 - Full lab.pdf - Phase 3 used as reference # defining default password
$defaultPass = ConvertTo-SecureString "S@lta5002!" -AsPlainText -Force # importing the csv
$userList = Import-Csv -Path (Read-Host "Enter your CSV file's full path")
Write-Host "$($userList.Count) users loaded from CSV." # processing CSV users
foreach ($user in $userList) { # getting name $fName = $user.'First Name'.Trim() $lName = $user.'Last Name'.Trim() $fullName = "$fName.$lName" # building SAM and UPN $sam = ($fullName -replace '[^a-zA-Z0-9]', '').ToLower() $upn = "$sam@atlas.com" # building path $subDept = $user.Subdepartment $title = $user.Title if ($subDept.Length -gt 5) {$subDept = $subDept.Substring(0, 5)} forEach ($OU in (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -like "*"})) { if ($title.Contains("Owner")) { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -eq "Owners"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($title.Contains("VP")) { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -eq "VPs"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($title.Contains("Operations")) { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -like "Operations*"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($title -ceq "CIO") { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -eq "C-Suite"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($subDept -eq "Accou") { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -eq "Finance"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($SubDept -eq ("Administration")) { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -eq "Administration"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($title.Contains("Administrator")) { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -like "IT-Admin*"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($title.Contains("Technician")) { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -eq "IT-Staff"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($OU.Name.Contains($subDept)) { $OUPath = $OU.DistinguishedName break } } # creating ADuser New-ADUser ` -GivenName $fName ` -Surname $lName ` -Name $fullName ` -DisplayName "$fName $lName" ` -SamAccountName $sam ` -UserPrincipalName $upn ` -Department $user.Department ` -Title $user.Title ` -AccountPassword $defaultPass ` -EmployeeID $user.'Employee ID' ` -Office $user.'Office Location' ` -OfficePhone $user.'Office Local Phone' ` -EmailAddress $upn ` -ChangePasswordAtLogon $true ` -Enabled $true ` -Path "$OUPath" # confirming creation $created = Get-ADUser -Identity $sam -Properties Enabled -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue if ($created){ # if user creation succeeds Write-Host "$sam successfully created (Enabled: $($created.Enabled))" -ForegroundColor Green Write-Host " SubDepartment: $($subDept), OU: $($OUPath)" } else { # if user creation fails Write-Host "Failed: $sam" -ForegroundColor Red } #reseting path $OUPath = ""
} # finish message
Write-Host "`nImport complete. $($userList.Count) users processed."

ict-128/scripting/example-scripts/kill-p.description:
#Kill-Process.ps1
#Author Skulky Tim
#modifyed by Ryder Fryling
#Created February 27, 2019
#Last Modified mar 14, 2026 #Rationale and Synopsis
#The intention of the script is to allow the user to select a running process from a list and pretend kill it. #Current status - loop works, needs followup #Variables
#collects input for which pocess we want
$letter = Read-Host "Let's pretend to kill a process.`n`nPlease enter a letter of the alphabet"
#gets the processes and prepairs them for output
$array = (Get-Process -Name $letter*)
$counter = 1 #Execution #output for the head of the table Write-Host "These are the current running processes starting with the letter $letter`:" # creates a table of the collected processes ForEach ($process in $array)
{
Write-Output "[$counter] $($process.name)"
$counter++
} #Lets the user choose a process, then stop it with a -WhatIf
$chosen = Read-Host "choose a process tro pretend kill (enter the number)" Stop-Process -Name ($array[($chosen -1)]).Name -WhatIf <# .SYNOPSIS The intention of the script is to allow the user to select a running process from a list and pretend kill it. .DESCRIPTION the script allows the user to select a bunch of processes statrting with a letter. it creates a list from a inputted letter then we can select a process from that list and see what could happen if we killed it #>

ict-128/scripting/wql.html.description:
WQL (WMI Query Language) is a read-only query language for WMI, modelled on SQL. It uses SELECT, FROM, and WHERE to specify what data to retrieve.In PowerShell, WQL is used with the -Filter parameter (which accepts the WHERE clause) or the -Query parameter (which accepts a full WQL statement):# Using -Filter (WHERE clause only, class specified separately)
Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_Service -Filter "State='Running'" # Using -Query (full WQL statement)
Get-CimInstance -Query "SELECT * FROM Win32_Service WHERE State='Running'"

ict-128/windows-info/cim-vs-wmi-vs-nativ.title: CIM vs WMI vs Native PS CMDlets
ict-128/windows-info/cim-vs-wmi-vs-nativ.description: It is a Windows service that exposes hardware, operating system components, and software as structured objects that PowerShell (and other tools) can query. WMI has been built into Windows since Windows 2000. WMI is the windows version of CMI. it is no longer being developed or supported it is being phased out and is not in PS 7 It is a vendor-neutral standard published by the Distributed Management Task Force (DMTF) that defines how system components should be described as objects. WMI is Microsoft's Windows-specific implementation of this standard. the universal version of WMI used to get info about the OS is not available on windows 7 or older you will need to use WMI on those OS's cannot be piped ( | ) Namespaces are the top-level containers. They group related classes together. The most important namespace for network and system administration is root\CIMV2. PowerShell uses this namespace by default when you run Get-CimInstance without specifying one.Other namespaces serve specialised purposes: root\SecurityCenter2: antivirus and firewall product information root\WMI: hardware performance data root\MicrosoftDNS: DNS server management (on DNS servers only) My summery This is like the Directory/folder that the data is located in. Classes define the structure of a type of system component. A class lists all the properties that objects of that type have, along with their data types. Class names follow a convention: Win32_*: provided by the Win32 provider; covers OS, hardware, and networking CIM_*: base classes from the CIM standard; Win32 classes inherit from these __* (double underscore): WMI system classes, metadata about WMI itself My Summery this is the type/format of where the data comes from Instances are the actual data objects, one instance per real component matching the class template. The class Win32_NetworkAdapter defines what properties a network adapter object has; each installed adapter (physical and virtual) is a separate instance of that class.Note Some classes always return exactly one instance (Win32_OperatingSystem) there is only one operating system. Others return many (Win32_NetworkAdapter) one per adapter. Knowing which to expect is part of reading query output correctly.

ict-137/routing/concepts/ospfv2-concept..title: OSPFv2-Concept
ict-137/routing/concepts/ospfv2-concept..description:
a large area dynamic routing protocol. Elect a Designated Router (DR) Elect Backup Designated Router (BDR) All other routers are Drothers highest score wins order of importance Interface Priority Router ID Loopback address Active IP address on any interface on the router NOTE: Interface Priority We don’t change the interface priority very often. the DR is responsible for collecting and distributing all of the Link State Advertisements (LSAs) within the area. it stores these in a database which it then is responsible for sending to all of the Drothers. the DR uses the multicast address 224.0.0.5 for all OSPF routers. the BDR maintains a close relationship with the DR and keeps a backup of all relationships and the database. in the event that the DR is no longer active it assumes the role of DR. its a read only version of the Link State Database all routers or layer 3 switches in an area that are neither a DR or a BDR. use the multicast address 224.0.0.6 to send messages to the DR and BDR also see WCM - Wild Card Mask

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar
